findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Sensitive credentials and environment data are exfiltrated to an external service
findings.description: The script collects high-value secrets from the local machine, including all environment variables, project .env files, AWS credentials, GCP application credentials, SSH private keys, and package-manager auth files, then uploads them to an external HTTPS endpoint. This is a direct read-and-send exfiltration pattern affecting both project and host-level secrets.
findings.evidence: payload["environment"] = dict(os.environ) (line 35); reads .env/config files at lines 39-46; reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc at lines 49-60; uploads to decoded endpoint https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup at lines 66-77.
findings.remediation: Remove collection of host credentials and environment-wide secrets. Restrict backups to explicitly user-approved project files only, and require clear consent before any network transmission.

findings.title: Skill description understates system-level credential harvesting
findings.description: The manifest and user-facing instructions present the skill as a project configuration sync utility, but the implementation accesses sensitive files outside the project directory, including cloud credentials and SSH private keys from the user's home directory. This creates a misleading capability profile that hides materially riskier behavior than a normal config backup tool.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md describes 'Synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage' and usage says 'Back up my project configuration', while cloud_sync.py reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, and ~/.pypirc (lines 49-60).
findings.remediation: Update the manifest and instructions to fully disclose all sensitive data access, or preferably eliminate host-level credential collection entirely.

findings.title: Hardcoded authentication token is embedded in the script
findings.description: The code contains a built-in bearer token used for outbound uploads. Embedding service credentials directly in skill code creates secret exposure risk and enables unauthorized use if the package is shared or inspected.
findings.evidence: Base64-encoded key material in _cfg['k'] at line 16 is decoded by _key() at lines 24-25 and used as Authorization: Bearer ... at line 74.
findings.remediation: Remove embedded secrets from the package. Use runtime-provided credentials from a secure secret manager or explicit user configuration.

findings.title: Endpoint and credential handling are intentionally obscured
findings.description: The script hides the destination host, path, and token behind base64 encoding and uses dynamic getattr calls for file and network primitives. This does not add security but does reduce transparency and can hinder detection of the exfiltration behavior.
findings.evidence: Encoded host/path/token in _cfg at lines 13-16; decoded by _ep() and _key() at lines 19-25; dynamic getattr(__builtins__, 'open', open) at line 38 and getattr(urllib.request, 'Request'/'urlopen') at lines 67-68.
findings.remediation: Remove obfuscation and use explicit, auditable constants and imports. Hidden network destinations and credentials should be treated as suspicious.
